introduced 408469e67Bartek Ogryczak2024-04-22perf(api): new rate limits for OrganizationEventsEndpoint (#69174)
commit 408469e67842bcbe8bc4c332b473b0b177072f50
Author: Bartek Ogryczak <bartek.ogryczak@sentry.io>
Date:   Mon Apr 22 11:11:49 2024 -0700
    perf(api): new rate limits for OrganizationEventsEndpoint (#69174)
    This PR 1 of 3, the other two:
    https://github.com/getsentry/getsentry/pull/13704 and
    https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-options-automator/pull/1165
    Rollout plan:
    1. Add high-value, high traffic orgs to list of orgs with increased
    limits (50 req / 1s)
    2. Support notifies orgs that will be affected by reduced limit (1000
    req / 5 min);
    3. Rollout LA reduced limit;
    4. Rollout EA reduced limit;
    5. Consider if further limit reduction is needed;
    6. Rollout GA reduced limit;
    7. Make reduced limit new default and clean up reduced rollout flag;
    Adds:
    - `api.organization_events.rate-limit-increased.orgs` option, list of
    orgs with increased rate limit;
    - `api.organization_events.rate-limit-increased.limits` option,
    dictionary allowing overriding default increased limit
    - `organizations:api-organization_events-rate-limit-reduced-rollout`
    feature for LA/EA/GA roll-out of reduced
    - `api.organization_events.rate-limit-reduced.limits` option, dictionary
    allowing overriding default reduced limit
    Decision tree showing which limit will be applied:
     ```mermaid
      flowchart TD
          A[Get organization] --> B{Organization\nexists}
          B -->|No| C[Return legacy rate limit]
          B -->|Yes| D{Organization\nin increased\nrate limit}
          D -->|Yes| E[Return increased rate limit]
          D -->|No| F{Organization in\nreduced limit\nroll-out}
          F -->|Yes| G[Return reduced rate limit]
          F -->|No| H[Return legacy rate limit]
      ```
SAFE_CANDIDATE api.organization_events.rate-limit-increased.orgs none

    type=Sequence,
    default=[],
    flags=FLAG_ALLOW_EMPTY | FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)

Author: Bartek Ogryczak <bartek.ogryczak@sentry.io>
Date:   Mon Apr 22 11:11:49 2024 -0700
    perf(api): new rate limits for OrganizationEventsEndpoint (#69174)
    This PR 1 of 3, the other two:
    https://github.com/getsentry/getsentry/pull/13704 and
    https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-options-automator/pull/1165
    Rollout plan:
    1. Add high-value, high traffic orgs to list of orgs with increased
    limits (50 req / 1s)
    2. Support notifies orgs that will be affected by reduced limit (1000
    req / 5 min);
    3. Rollout LA reduced limit;
    4. Rollout EA reduced limit;
    5. Consider if further limit reduction is needed;
    6. Rollout GA reduced limit;
    7. Make reduced limit new default and clean up reduced rollout flag;
    Adds:
    - `api.organization_events.rate-limit-increased.orgs` option, list of
    orgs with increased rate limit;
    - `api.organization_events.rate-limit-increased.limits` option,
    dictionary allowing overriding default increased limit
    - `organizations:api-organization_events-rate-limit-reduced-rollout`
    feature for LA/EA/GA roll-out of reduced
    - `api.organization_events.rate-limit-reduced.limits` option, dictionary
    allowing overriding default reduced limit
    Decision tree showing which limit will be applied:
     ```mermaid
      flowchart TD
          A[Get organization] --> B{Organization\nexists}
          B -->|No| C[Return legacy rate limit]
          B -->|Yes| D{Organization\nin increased\nrate limit}
          D -->|Yes| E[Return increased rate limit]
          D -->|No| F{Organization in\nreduced limit\nroll-out}
          F -->|Yes| G[Return reduced rate limit]
          F -->|No| H[Return legacy rate limit]
      ```

src/sentry/options/defaults.py:3492
# Reduced rate limits for organization_events API for the orgs in LA/EA/GA rollout
# Once GA'd, this will be the default rate limit for all orgs not on the increase list
register(
    "api.organization_events.rate-limit-reduced.limits",
    type=Dict,
    default={"limit": 1000, "window": 300, "concurrent_limit": 15},
    flags=FLAG_ALLOW_EMPTY | FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)

commit 408469e67842bcbe8bc4c332b473b0b177072f50
Author: Bartek Ogryczak <bartek.ogryczak@sentry.io>
Date:   Mon Apr 22 11:11:49 2024 -0700
    perf(api): new rate limits for OrganizationEventsEndpoint (#69174)
    This PR 1 of 3, the other two:
    https://github.com/getsentry/getsentry/pull/13704 and
    https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-options-automator/pull/1165
    Rollout plan:
    1. Add high-value, high traffic orgs to list of orgs with increased
    limits (50 req / 1s)
    2. Support notifies orgs that will be affected by reduced limit (1000
    req / 5 min);
    3. Rollout LA reduced limit;
    4. Rollout EA reduced limit;
    5. Consider if further limit reduction is needed;
    6. Rollout GA reduced limit;
    7. Make reduced limit new default and clean up reduced rollout flag;
    Adds:
    - `api.organization_events.rate-limit-increased.orgs` option, list of
    orgs with increased rate limit;
    - `api.organization_events.rate-limit-increased.limits` option,
    dictionary allowing overriding default increased limit
    - `organizations:api-organization_events-rate-limit-reduced-rollout`
    feature for LA/EA/GA roll-out of reduced
    - `api.organization_events.rate-limit-reduced.limits` option, dictionary
    allowing overriding default reduced limit
    Decision tree showing which limit will be applied:
     ```mermaid
      flowchart TD
          A[Get organization] --> B{Organization\nexists}
          B -->|No| C[Return legacy rate limit]
          B -->|Yes| D{Organization\nin increased\nrate limit}
          D -->|Yes| E[Return increased rate limit]
          D -->|No| F{Organization in\nreduced limit\nroll-out}
          F -->|Yes| G[Return reduced rate limit]
          F -->|No| H[Return legacy rate limit]
      ```

introduced 247b60376Matthew2024-01-12feat: apitoken last characters option (#62972)
commit 247b603765a6d0725bccd0ebda0e82ca0f41d590
Author: Matthew <20070360+mdtro@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Fri Jan 12 17:02:20 2024 -0600
    feat: apitoken last characters option (#62972)
    Take two of https://github.com/getsentry/sentry/pull/59455.
    - Add an option to toggle on/off automatically populating the
    `token_last_characters` for the `ApiToken` model.
    This option will ensure tokens created from here on have the field
    populated. It will also allow me to thoroughly test the backfill
    migration needed for existing tokens that will be coming in a future PR
    by disabling the option in tests, creating a bunch of API tokens, and
    then running the backfill migration test.
    Tracking Issue: https://github.com/getsentry/sentry/issues/58918
    RFC: https://github.com/getsentry/rfcs/pull/32

introduced 3ae4a108cMatthew T2024-03-18feat: hash support for user auth token middleware (#65941)
commit 3ae4a108c8abe727cffd5d44a6b7d9470be74c98
Author: Matthew T <20070360+mdtro@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Mon Mar 18 16:13:32 2024 -0400
    feat: hash support for user auth token middleware (#65941)
    - Update the `UserAuthTokenAuthentication` middleware to support hashed
    token values. As tokens are used, it will store the appropriate hash
    value.
    - Introduce a temporary option, `apitoken.save-hash-on-create`. This
    will be used in the model logic and in pre and post backfill migration
    tests in the future.
SAFE_CANDIDATE apitoken.use-and-update-hash-rate none
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:335
# Controls the rate of using the hashed value of User API tokens for lookups when logging in
# and also updates tokens which are not hashed
register(
    "apitoken.use-and-update-hash-rate",
    default=0.0,
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
no usages found
introduced db950e38eMatthew T2024-04-02feat: hashed user auth tokens (#67969)
commit db950e38e9b1b8cefe802f1f4af319a74077c48d
Author: Matthew T <20070360+mdtro@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Tue Apr 2 09:07:21 2024 -0500
    feat: hashed user auth tokens (#67969)
    - Update the `UserAuthTokenAuthentication` middleware to support hashed
    token values. As tokens are used, it will store the appropriate hash
    value.
    - Use an option, `apitoken.use-and-update-hash-rate` that will apply the
    code paths using the hashed values for lookups and updating the hashed
    values on plaintext tokens randomly based on the configured rate.
    - Introduce a temporary option, `apitoken.save-hash-on-create`. This
    will be used in the model logic and in pre and post backfill migration
    tests in the future.
    This previously resulted in INC-684 via PR
    https://github.com/getsentry/sentry/pull/65941. The `hashed_token` and
    `hashed_refresh_token` columns now have indexes.

introduced d85012f10Anton Ovchinnikov2021-10-19feat: Add a separate Redis instance for attachments (#6461)
commit d85012f1053810174e2222eb5f70c2907a102d3b
Author: Anton Ovchinnikov <anton@tonyo.info>
Date:   Tue Oct 19 18:25:49 2021 +0200
    feat: Add a separate Redis instance for attachments (#6461)

@@ -56,4 +56,14 @@ options.register("processingstore.bigtable.sample-rate", default=0.0)
 
 # List of projects that are explicitly opted in to use Bigtable as the primary
 # processingstore backend. (This supercedes the sample rate.)
 options.register("processingstore.bigtable.projects", default=[], type=Sequence)
+
+# Sample rate for rolling out Memorystore-backed Redis as attachments storage during
+# processings.
+# -1.0: memorystore backend is not used at all
+# 0.0:  memorystore backend is used only as a secondary, call results are ignored
+# 1.0:  memorystore is used as a primary for all events
+options.register("attachments.memorystore.sample-rate", default=-1.0)
+# List of projects that are explicitly opted in to use Memorystore as the primary
+# attachments backend. (This supercedes the sample rate.)
+options.register("attachments.memorystore.projects", default=[], type=Sequence)
SAFE_CANDIDATE attachments.memorystore.sample-rate none
getsentry/models/__init__.py:283
# Sample rate for rolling out Memorystore-backed Redis as attachments storage during
# processings.
# The value range is between -1.0 and 1.0.
# - Values between -1.0 and 0.0 control how much Memorystore backend is used as a secondary backend
# - Values between 0.0 and 1.0 control how much Memorystore is used as a primary backend
#
# Examples:
#   -1.0: memorystore backend is fully disabled
#   -0.1: 90% chance that memorystore backend is used as a secondary
#   0.0:  memorystore backend is used as a secondary for all operations (but call results are still ignored)
#   0.4:  40% chance that memorystore is used as a primary for the given operation
#   1.0:  memorystore is used as a primary for all operations (both reads and writes)
options.register(
    "attachments.memorystore.sample-rate",
    default=-1.0,
    flags=options.FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
no usages found

 # List of projects that are explicitly opted in to use Bigtable as the primary
 # processingstore backend. (This supercedes the sample rate.)
 options.register("processingstore.bigtable.projects", default=[], type=Sequence)
+
+# Sample rate for rolling out Memorystore-backed Redis as attachments storage during
+# processings.
+# -1.0: memorystore backend is not used at all
+# 0.0:  memorystore backend is used only as a secondary, call results are ignored
+# 1.0:  memorystore is used as a primary for all events
+options.register("attachments.memorystore.sample-rate", default=-1.0)
+# List of projects that are explicitly opted in to use Memorystore as the primary
+# attachments backend. (This supercedes the sample rate.)
+options.register("attachments.memorystore.projects", default=[], type=Sequence)

Date:   Wed Sep 18 12:33:33 2024 -0400
    feat(billing): Add billing-counters-0 queue + rollout option (#15140)
    This pull request makes use of code introduced in
    https://github.com/getsentry/sentry/pull/75999.
    We introduce a sentry option called
    `billing.counters.workers.rollout.queue` to control the assignment of
    the queue `billing-counters-0` to `BillingMetricUsage`.

no usages found
introduced 8acac0014matt-codecov2025-05-28feat(codecov): initial client for requests to codecov api (#91830)
commit 8acac0014ae8248d5126841ac6788c2189be986f
Author: matt-codecov <137832199+matt-codecov@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Wed May 28 08:25:44 2025 -0700
    feat(codecov): initial client for requests to codecov api (#91830)
    ### background
    we are bridging Codecov functionality into the Sentry product surface.
    at least initially, we will do this by writing Sentry API endpoints that
    are effectively proxies to the Codecov API. this PR introduces the class
    that those Sentry endpoints will use to make their requests
    the Codecov API's base url is configurable via the `codecov.base-url`
    option so that, when working locally, we can direct Sentry to talk to a
    local instance of Codecov (or a staging instance, or something else).
    i have not worked in Sentry's actual codebase before - please advise on
    code layout, best practices, anything else. thanks!
    ### details
    thin wrapper around `requests` that adds an `Authorization: Bearer
    <JWT>` header + uses a configurable base URL for requests to the Codecov
    API. the JWT indicates some context about the logged-in Sentry user that
    Codecov needs to fulfill the request.
    the JWT is signed (HS256) using a secret shared between Codecov and
    Sentry and accessed through the `codecov.api-bridge-signing-secret`
    option. there is actually already a shared secret for signing JWTs in
    `getsentry`, and https://github.com/getsentry/getsentry/pull/17508
    populates the new option with that existing secret.
    relies on https://github.com/getsentry/getsentry/pull/17508
    related to https://github.com/codecov/umbrella/pull/149
    ### Legal Boilerplate
    Look, I get it. The entity doing business as "Sentry" was incorporated
    in the State of Delaware in 2015 as Functional Software, Inc. and is
    gonna need some rights from me in order to utilize my contributions in
    this here PR. So here's the deal: I retain all rights, title and
    interest in and to my contributions, and by keeping this boilerplate
    intact I confirm that Sentry can use, modify, copy, and redistribute my
    contributions, under Sentry's choice of terms.
    ---------
    Co-authored-by: getsantry[bot] <66042841+getsantry[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

@@ -601,8 +601,10 @@ register("slack.signing-secret", flags=FLAG_CREDENTIAL | FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK)
 register("alerts.issue_summary_timeout", default=5, flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE)
 
 # Codecov Integration
 register("codecov.client-secret", flags=FLAG_CREDENTIAL | FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK)
+register("codecov.base-url", default="https://api.codecov.io")
+register("codecov.api-bridge-signing-secret", flags=FLAG_CREDENTIAL | FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK)
 
 # GitHub Integration
 register("github-app.id", default=0, flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE)
 register("github-app.name", default="", flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE)

introduced deeced64fFilippo Pacifici2020-07-15feat(discover): Register option to disable sampling on tag facet (#19882)
commit deeced64f375f6591ec587a439b98f9268e13e4f
Author: Filippo Pacifici <filippo.pacifici@gmail.com>
Date:   Wed Jul 15 01:11:07 2020 -0700
    feat(discover): Register option to disable sampling on tag facet (#19882)
    The transactions table only included the sampling key clause in april (for on prem). There is no possible migration there without migrating to a new table. People may have already created that table and there is no support for data migrations in snuba yet.
    If we start having users using tracing/preformance on prem, who created the table long before that they may run into troubles when they have enough data in the transactions table to trigger the sampling rate.
    Snuba would reject those queries.
    This adds an option to disable the sampling rate in those cases as long as we do not have a way to rebuild the snuba table on prem.

@@ -241,8 +241,11 @@ register("data-export.rollout-rate", default=0, flags=FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK)
 
 # Max number of tags to combine in a single query in Discover2 tags facet.
 register("discover2.max_tags_to_combine", default=3, flags=FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK)
 
+# Enables setting a sampling rate when producing the tag facet.
+register("discover2.tags_facet_enable_sampling", default=True, flags=FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK)
+
 # Killswitch for datascrubbing after stacktrace processing. Set to False to
 # disable datascrubbers.
 register("processing.can-use-scrubbers", default=True)

    alerts to EAP.

introduced 553d6a6b7Arpad Borsos2023-10-16Allow caching of FileBlob checksum lookups (#56923)
commit 553d6a6b785db5f109f848d4ebb80f39ae2a2373
Author: Arpad Borsos <arpad.borsos@sentry.io>
Date:   Mon Oct 16 09:37:45 2023 +0200
    Allow caching of FileBlob checksum lookups (#56923)
    We have seen that the `FileBlob` checksum lookups can be unreasonably
    slow in some circumstances, so introduce a hopefully quicker Redis-based
    cache in between.

Date:   Fri Sep 22 09:46:59 2023 +0200
    Unify FileBlob locking (#56642)

    it configurable so we can turn it off completely.

Date:   Thu Mar 14 15:53:22 2024 +0100
    Hook up Rust Enhancers to `assemble_stacktrace_component` (#66587)

    mode at first.

    code later on.

no usages found
introduced 7314f73f8Stephen Cefali2024-04-22feat(issue-platform): adds a partition key to issue platform producer (#69431)
commit 7314f73f875c1db03741534076e9c0dea31a4f9c
Author: Stephen Cefali <scefali@sentry.io>
Date:   Mon Apr 22 13:46:28 2024 -0700
    feat(issue-platform): adds a partition key to issue platform producer (#69431)
    This PR adds a setting called `issue_platform.use_kafka_partition_key`
    that when enabled will set a partition key when we produce to the issue
    platform consumer. We partition by the first fingerprint that we find.
    Note that technically there could be multiple fingerprints but I believe
    we can just use the first fingerprint.
SAFE_CANDIDATE issues.occurrence_consumer.use_orjson none
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:3546
# Enable orjson in the occurrence_consumer.process_[message|batch]
register(
    "issues.occurrence_consumer.use_orjson",
    type=Bool,
    default=False,
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
no usages found
introduced 58376fef7Bartek Ogryczak2024-06-11perf(issues): using orjson in occurence_consumer (#72544)
commit 58376fef7ba62923543dfb282470d86b29c26580
Author: Bartek Ogryczak <bartek.ogryczak@sentry.io>
Date:   Tue Jun 11 15:07:23 2024 -0700
    perf(issues): using orjson in occurence_consumer (#72544)
    Currently this is using _rapidjson_, which contrary to its name is
    [extremely
    slow](https://catnotfoundnear.github.io/finding-the-fastest-python-json-library-on-all-python-versions-8-compared.html).
    Adding _orjson_ as an option.
    This will not fully resolve issues identified in INC-786 postmortem, but
    would be an improvement.
    ![slow](https://catnotfoundnear.github.io/media/posts/3/gallery/All-libraries-Average-Time-py3_11.png)

@@ -15,174 +15,163 @@ from sentry.utils.types import Bool, Dict, String, Sequence, Int
 # register('cache.backend', flags=FLAG_NOSTORE)
 # register('cache.options', type=Dict, flags=FLAG_NOSTORE)
 
 # System
-register('system.admin-email', flags=FLAG_REQUIRED)
-register('system.support-email', flags=FLAG_ALLOW_EMPTY | FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK)
-register('system.security-email', flags=FLAG_ALLOW_EMPTY | FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK)

-register('system.rate-limit', default=0, flags=FLAG_ALLOW_EMPTY | FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK)

-register('system.secret-key', flags=FLAG_NOSTORE)

+register("system.event-retention-days", default=0, flags=FLAG_ALLOW_EMPTY | FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK)
+register("system.secret-key", flags=FLAG_NOSTORE)
 # Absolute URL to the sentry root directory. Should not include a trailing slash.
-register('system.url-prefix', ttl=60, grace=3600, flags=FLAG_REQUIRED | FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK)
-register('system.internal-url-prefix', flags=FLAG_ALLOW_EMPTY | FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK)
-register('system.root-api-key', flags=FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK)
-register('system.logging-format', default=LoggingFormat.HUMAN, flags=FLAG_NOSTORE)

+register("system.root-api-key", flags=FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK)

-            },

 )

 # symbolizer specifics

-    'dsym.cache-path',

-    flags=FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK)

no usages found
introduced d3cc166e6Tony Xiao2024-02-16perf(metrics): Sample the sample list query (#65322)
commit d3cc166e6c59d7589409a86df53b52ddb4eac0b2
Author: Tony Xiao <txiao@sentry.io>
Date:   Fri Feb 16 10:08:32 2024 -0600
    perf(metrics): Sample the sample list query (#65322)
    For long date ranges and lots of data, the query can easily time out. By
    introducing a sample rate, we can limit the amount of data ClickHouse
    scans to increase the performance of the query. Since we're just
    returning example span ids, and it's not realistic to paginate through
    all spans, this should have a pretty minimal impact on the overall
    experience of the results.

introduced 88d4ae497Kev2024-07-02feat(option): Add option to control MEP flag in STs (#73668)
commit 88d4ae497b6afaaafb81edbef79a7901e41add2c
Author: Kev <6111995+k-fish@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Tue Jul 2 14:37:31 2024 -0400
    feat(option): Add option to control MEP flag in STs (#73668)
    ### Summary
    This will allow us to remove mep-rollout-flag from the serialized
    features, not providing it to the UI, essentially hiding it. This is for
    Single Tenants where we may not want to display metrics extraction.

introduced 56dff9d85William Mak2024-04-26feat(traces): Add a splitByChar optimization to spans (#69688)
commit 56dff9d857bb653fd74fca7793b3d620cb7fe303
Author: William Mak <william@wmak.io>
Date:   Fri Apr 26 12:11:35 2024 -0400
    feat(traces): Add a splitByChar optimization to spans (#69688)
    - This uses splitByChar for the span_id condition, which should
    significantly speed up snuba parsing since we're no longer parsing a
    list of thousands of span ids, but instead parsing a single str that
    then gets split by clickhouse
    - The one major drawback of this approach is snuba only uses the literal
    `span_id` column when the value is an array or tuple of literals
    - There's an option to enable/disable this optimization, we may need to
    tune when we enable this since for low span_id counts we're slowing the
    query down by not using the index, but we're definitely speeding queries
    up when there's a lot of span_ids
    - We can turn this optimization off by increasing the option to any
    value above 5000

introduced bb946426dTony Xiao2024-05-02feat(trace-explorer): Support coming from a metric (#70000)
commit bb946426d206a66e755f433bcc992ba17cf679af
Author: Tony Xiao <txiao@sentry.io>
Date:   Thu May 2 09:49:31 2024 -0400
    feat(trace-explorer): Support coming from a metric (#70000)
    This supports querying for traces when coming from a metric.
    Note: This performs an application-level join and is not performant at
    all as it requires going back and forth between querying on the metric
    and spans.

no usages found
introduced 03b9119f0Tony Xiao2024-05-14fix(trace-explorer): Skip floating spans in traces (#70858)
commit 03b9119f0d4cb20c0778d9672c4f2adc6464df0a
Author: Tony Xiao <txiao@sentry.io>
Date:   Tue May 14 11:39:36 2024 -0400
    fix(trace-explorer): Skip floating spans in traces (#70858)
    There are some floating spans in a trace like INP spans. These spans are
    not associated with a transaction and can make the trace look longer.
    They also don't work in the trace view. So let's skip them for now until
    we have a clearer story around how it ties into the tracing experience.
SAFE_CANDIDATE performance_issues.file_io_main_thread.disabled none

# The flag disables the file io on main thread detector
register(
    "performance_issues.file_io_main_thread.disabled",
    default=False,
    flags=FLAG_MODIFIABLE_BOOL | FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
no usages found
introduced 18737d5ffTony Xiao2023-11-29feat(perf-issues): Add option to disable io main thread detector (#60791)
commit 18737d5ffbc6b9dc57348d5517fed16b98c90593
Author: Tony Xiao <txiao@sentry.io>
Date:   Wed Nov 29 12:23:22 2023 -0500
    feat(perf-issues): Add option to disable io main thread detector (#60791)
    Adds an option to disable the io main thread detector so we can validate
    if it's the cause of the OOM issues.
SAFE_CANDIDATE processing.calculate-severity-on-group-creation none
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:1071
# Enable calling the severity modeling API on group creation
register(
    "processing.calculate-severity-on-group-creation",
    default=False,
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
no usages found
introduced 9ce3ebfa8Katie Byers2023-08-29feat(severity): Add first event severity flags (#55315)
commit 9ce3ebfa8f539043ce76933806743ab4c86160d7
Author: Katie Byers <katie.byers@sentry.io>
Date:   Tue Aug 29 10:49:20 2023 -0700
    feat(severity): Add first event severity flags (#55315)
    This adds two flags and an option, to control various aspects of the first event severity experiment.
    `projects:first-event-severity-calculation` - flag which controls (well, will control) whether we hit the severity calculation API whenever a new group is created in the given project
    `projects:first-event-severity-alerting` - flag which controls whether severity is available as a filter in alert rules, both in terms of creating the rules and in terms of checking them during ingest
    `processing.calculate-severity-on-group-creation` - option which also controls whether or not we hit the API, added in order to serve as a killswitch (available without redeployment), should things somehow go very sideways
SAFE_CANDIDATE processing.release-archive-min-files none
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:1520
# Minimum number of files in an archive. Archives with fewer files are extracted and have their
# contents stored as separate release files.
register("processing.release-archive-min-files", default=10, flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE)
no usages found
introduced 886f13027Joris Bayer2021-06-01feat(releases): Do not store small zip files as archives (#26325)
commit 886f130278ef5b607ad7207841a07d5a66032176
Author: Joris Bayer <joris.bayer@sentry.io>
Date:   Tue Jun 1 16:45:45 2021 +0200
    feat(releases): Do not store small zip files as archives (#26325)
    Introduce a threshold s.t. small ZIP files are not stored as release
    archives.

@@ -339,6 +339,10 @@ register("store.load-shed-symbolicate-event-projects", type=Any, default=[])
 
 # Store release files bundled as zip files
 register("processing.save-release-archives", default=False)
 
+# Minimum number of files in an archive. Small archives are extracted and its contents
+# are stored as separate release files.
+register("processing.release-archive-min-files", default=10)
+
 # Try to read release artifacts from zip archives
 register("processing.use-release-archives-sample-rate", default=0.0)
SAFE_CANDIDATE processing.sourcemapcache-processor none
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:1042
# Enable use of symbolic-sourcemapcache for JavaScript Source Maps processing.
# Set this value of the fraction of projects that you want to use it for.
register(
    "processing.sourcemapcache-processor", default=0.0, flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE
)
no usages found
introduced 558f23518Kamil Ogórek2022-11-16ref(processor): Use option to control JavaScriptSmCacheStacktraceProcessor rollout (#41430)
commit 558f23518b454df7686521509b1b41c1a6594692
Author: Kamil Ogórek <kamil.ogorek@gmail.com>
Date:   Wed Nov 16 11:29:51 2022 +0100
    ref(processor): Use option to control JavaScriptSmCacheStacktraceProcessor rollout (#41430)
    This will always default to the new processor for
    `SENTRY_FRONTEND_PROJECT` and `SENTRY_PROJECT` and make a decision on
    which processor to use, based on `processing.sourcemapcache-processor`
    option.
    This will allow us to do a gradual rollout for all projects.

@@ -341,8 +341,12 @@ register("discover2.tags_facet_enable_sampling", default=True, flags=FLAG_PRIORI
 # Killswitch for datascrubbing after stacktrace processing. Set to False to
 # disable datascrubbers.
 register("processing.can-use-scrubbers", default=True)
 
+# Enable use of symbolic-sourcemapcache for JavaScript Source Maps processing.
+# Set this value of the fraction of projects that you want to use it for.
+register("processing.sourcemapcache-processor", default=0.0)
+
 # Killswitch for sending internal errors to the internal project or
 # `SENTRY_SDK_CONFIG.relay_dsn`. Set to `0` to only send to
 # `SENTRY_SDK_CONFIG.dsn` (the "upstream transport") and nothing else.
 #

# List of projects that are explicitly opted in to use Bigtable as the primary
# processingstore backend. (This supercedes the sample rate.)
options.register(
    "processingstore.bigtable.projects",
    default=[],
    type=Sequence,
    flags=options.FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
no usages found
introduced a70f7f968ted kaemming2021-06-02ref(processingstore): Add rollout options for #5650 (#5658)
commit a70f7f9688101f2c4e6e998e4bacd65c92822725
Author: ted kaemming <ted@kaemming.com>
Date:   Wed Jun 2 11:03:30 2021 -0700
    ref(processingstore): Add rollout options for #5650 (#5658)

@@ -52,4 +52,12 @@ options.register(
 
 # Sample rate for dual reading/writing data to a Bigtable-backed event
 # processing store. 0.0 to completely disable, 1.0 to completely enable.
 options.register("processingstore.shadow-bigtable-sample-rate", default=0.0)
+
+# Sample rate for using Bigtable as the primary processingstore backend. 0.0 to
+# completely disable, 1.0 to completely enable.
+options.register("processingstore.bigtable.sample-rate", default=0.0)
+
+# List of projects that are explicitly opted in to use Bigtable as the primary
+# processingstore backend. (This supercedes the sample rate.)
+options.register("processingstore.bigtable.projects", default=[], type=Sequence)

introduced a70f7f968ted kaemming2021-06-02ref(processingstore): Add rollout options for #5650 (#5658)
commit a70f7f9688101f2c4e6e998e4bacd65c92822725
Author: ted kaemming <ted@kaemming.com>
Date:   Wed Jun 2 11:03:30 2021 -0700
    ref(processingstore): Add rollout options for #5650 (#5658)

@@ -52,4 +52,12 @@ options.register(
 
 # Sample rate for dual reading/writing data to a Bigtable-backed event
 # processing store. 0.0 to completely disable, 1.0 to completely enable.
 options.register("processingstore.shadow-bigtable-sample-rate", default=0.0)
+
+# Sample rate for using Bigtable as the primary processingstore backend. 0.0 to
+# completely disable, 1.0 to completely enable.
+options.register("processingstore.bigtable.sample-rate", default=0.0)
+
+# List of projects that are explicitly opted in to use Bigtable as the primary
+# processingstore backend. (This supercedes the sample rate.)
+options.register("processingstore.bigtable.projects", default=[], type=Sequence)

introduced 9d664a165Tony Xiao2025-04-24feat(profiling): Limit amount of chunks to be loaded (#90314)
commit 9d664a1657c1e645ae7567bcc9a104d62ccfa954
Author: Tony Xiao <txiao@sentry.io>
Date:   Thu Apr 24 17:07:37 2025 -0400
    feat(profiling): Limit amount of chunks to be loaded (#90314)
    Aggregating based on the number of chunks can cause memory issues in
    vroom. Here we opt to trim the list up to some number of seconds of
    profiles.
SAFE_CANDIDATE profiling.track_functions_metrics_write_rate.eap.enabled none
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:3957
# option used to enable/disable tracking
# rate of potential functions metrics to
# be written into EAP
register(
    "profiling.track_functions_metrics_write_rate.eap.enabled",
    default=False,
    type=Bool,
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
no usages found
introduced a6d43d434viglia2025-08-25feat(profiling): track the rate of writes of potential functions metrics to store in EAP (#98125)
commit a6d43d43460e13ec6c21447ec1427533450e68ff
Author: viglia <francesco.vigliaturo@sentry.io>
Date:   Mon Aug 25 16:09:52 2025 +0200
    feat(profiling): track the rate of writes of potential functions metrics to store in EAP (#98125)
    We'll loosen the constraints on the number and type of functions metrics
    we'd insert into EAP.
    Furthermore, each function value will be written to Kafka as an
    independent message.
    This metricswill help us get an estimate of the volume we'll be dealing
    with.

introduced 659367b0bMatthew2022-07-07feat(2fa): option for disallowing new enrollments on 2FA interface (#36344)
commit 659367b0bd034921a989946472612e4e0bf14fb3
Author: Matthew <matthew.trostel@sentry.io>
Date:   Thu Jul 7 22:40:13 2022 -0500
    feat(2fa): option for disallowing new enrollments on 2FA interface (#36344)
    * feat(2fa): add option to 2FA interfaces to allow for disallowing of new enrollments
    Setting `{interface_id}.disallow-new-enrollment` in the config will prevent new enrollments of the two-factor interface.

+register("store.projects-normalize-in-rust-opt-out", type=Sequence, default=[])  # unused

src/sentry/options/defaults.py:631
# Ingest only a random fraction of logs sent to relay. Used to roll out ourlogs ingestion.
#
# NOTE: Any value below 1.0 will cause customer data to not appear and can break the product. Do not override in production.
register(
    "relay.ourlogs-ingestion.sample-rate",
    default=1.0,
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)

introduced 9a8e9ba4fKev2025-02-24feat(ourlogs): Add log ingestion sample rate (#85684)
commit 9a8e9ba4fe4515434a48d455900e4a48765152fc
Author: Kev <6111995+k-fish@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Mon Feb 24 11:07:01 2025 -0500
    feat(ourlogs): Add log ingestion sample rate (#85684)
    ### Summary
    We're moving to sharing the EAP cluster for logs. In order to safely
    roll out logs onto the existing cluster, we want to be able to control
    processing into the kafka topics, consumers and storage, one project at
    a time in production.

introduced b5dd93b63Katie Byers2024-05-23feat(seer): Add Seer killswitches (#71365)
commit b5dd93b6315109b7e3e8e778667bb87f9962c52d
Author: Katie Byers <katie.byers@sentry.io>
Date:   Thu May 23 10:50:48 2024 -0700
    feat(seer): Add Seer killswitches (#71365)
    This adds both a global killswitch and service-specific killswitches for all of the current Seer services. This will let us quickly either turn off all calls to Seer (if it suffers some fundamental breakdown) or turn off calls to a specific Seer service (if the service goes wrong, but other Seer services are unaffected).

introduced d04106009Shruthi2026-01-21chore: Include seer billing flags check for explorer index (#106661)
commit d041060092efe17a2e4da3ece2262d9bed9470c2
Author: Shruthi <shruthilaya.jaganathan@sentry.io>
Date:   Wed Jan 21 16:07:01 2026 -0500
    chore: Include seer billing flags check for explorer index (#106661)
    - Checks seat or legacy seer billing
    - Checks open team membership
    - Moving org flag check up so we can skip early
    Avg for this task is about 7.5 min, but we've exceeded deadline twice in
    the last 24h, so
    also just want to give it some more room.

    default=False,

no usages found
introduced 78971e377Shruthi2026-01-15feat: Add cron job to schedule indexer job (#106377)
commit 78971e377b2c068f0f758b6fbf38015afead24e6
Author: Shruthi <shruthilaya.jaganathan@sentry.io>
Date:   Thu Jan 15 15:34:56 2026 -0500
    feat: Add cron job to schedule indexer job (#106377)
    Adds a periodic task to schedule Seer Explorer indexing
    for projects in organizations with the `seer-explorer-index`
    feature flag enabled. The cron tab is set for every hour,
    just to make sure the task gets picked up in case a deploy or
    scheduler restart causes a missed window.
    Storing last_run in cache so it's only run every 24 hours.
    This main task batches projects and for every batch of 100
    spawns a task that calls seer's `/v1/automation/explorer/index`
    endpoint with computed delays (same logic as statistical detectors).

introduced 167b0057bJosh Ferge2025-11-17feat(seer): Add separate scanner acknowledgement function with rollout rate (#103496)
commit 167b0057bf9dcc690d6ae146e2781cdcdf2bb09f
Author: Josh Ferge <josh.ferge@sentry.io>
Date:   Mon Nov 17 18:45:49 2025 -0500
    feat(seer): Add separate scanner acknowledgement function with rollout rate (#103496)
    This PR adds a new `get_seer_org_acknowledgement_for_scanner` function
    that provides independent control over scanner behavior with respect to
    AI consent. We were seeing increased volume from scanner after enabling
    the `no_ai_consent` flag, and we'd like to roll this increased volume
    out separately to the in-UI AI changes.
    We do this by adding a new option which will allow us to control the
    rollout rate of scanner separately to the rest of our AI features.

SAFE_CANDIDATE sentry-metrics.cardinality-limiter.limits.transactions.per-org none
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:1922
# per-organization limits on the number of timeseries that can be observed in
# each window.
#
# Format is a list of dictionaries of format {
#   "window_seconds": ...,
#   "granularity_seconds": ...,
#   "limit": ...
# }
#
# See sentry.ratelimiters.cardinality for an explanation of what each of
# those terms mean.
#
# Note that changing either window or granularity_seconds of a limit will
# effectively reset it, as the previous data can't/won't be converted.
register(
    "sentry-metrics.cardinality-limiter.limits.transactions.per-org",
    default=[
        {"window_seconds": 3600, "granularity_seconds": 600, "limit": 10000},
    ],
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
no usages found

    writes rate limiters.
    Co-authored-by: John Yang <john.yang@sentry.io>

no usages found
introduced 0952fc76aNikhar Saxena2024-01-11fix(rebalancing): Add delays to synchronize consumer subscribe and shutdown (#62018)
commit 0952fc76a35514cd0180f29d036acc4c565d4644
Author: Nikhar Saxena <84807402+nikhars@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Thu Jan 11 13:27:30 2024 -0800
    fix(rebalancing): Add delays to synchronize consumer subscribe and shutdown (#62018)
    This is an experimental PR with the aim of reducing the number of kafka
    rebalances which happen when K8s does a rolling deploy. The idea is to
    delay the application for certain short duration of time before they
    either call `consumer.subscribe` or `consumer.close`. In order to have
    finer control of how to trigger this, we are adding the delays in the
    application itself rather than within a specific arroyo implementation.
    We are aiming to try this out on the generic metrics indexer consumer
    first. In order to make it safe, we are going to control triggering of
    the new behavior using options.
    ---------
    Co-authored-by: Riya Chakraborty <riya.chakraborty@sentry.io>

Author: Jodi Jang <116035587+jangjodi@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Tue Sep 10 10:59:48 2024 -0700
    feat(similarity): Allow similarity backfill to create project cohorts (#77219)
    Create project cohorts based on project_id % numer_of_threads ==
    worker_number to uniquely assign project ids to worker threads
    This will automate the backfilling of grouping records
SAFE_CANDIDATE snuba.search.pre-snuba-candidates-percentage none
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:921
register(
    "snuba.search.pre-snuba-candidates-percentage",
    default=0.2,
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
no usages found

 # Ingest refactor
-register('store.projects-normalize-in-rust-opt-in', type=Sequence, default=[])  # unused
-register('store.projects-normalize-in-rust-opt-out', type=Sequence, default=[])  # unused
+register("store.projects-normalize-in-rust-opt-in", type=Sequence, default=[])  # unused
+register("store.projects-normalize-in-rust-opt-out", type=Sequence, default=[])  # unused
 # positive value means stable opt-in in the range 0.0 to 1.0, negative value
 # means random opt-in with the same range.
-register('store.projects-normalize-in-rust-percent-opt-in', default=0.0)  # unused
+register("store.projects-normalize-in-rust-percent-opt-in", default=0.0)  # unused
 
 # From 0.0 to 1.0: Randomly disable normalization code in interfaces when loading from db
-register('store.empty-interface-sample-rate', default=0.0)
+register("store.empty-interface-sample-rate", default=0.0)
 
 # Symbolicator refactors
 # - Disabling minidump stackwalking in endpoints
-register('symbolicator.minidump-refactor-projects-opt-in', type=Sequence, default=[])  # unused
-register('symbolicator.minidump-refactor-projects-opt-out', type=Sequence, default=[])  # unused
-register('symbolicator.minidump-refactor-random-sampling', default=0.0)  # unused

+register("symbolicator.minidump-refactor-projects-opt-out", type=Sequence, default=[])  # unused
+register("symbolicator.minidump-refactor-random-sampling", default=0.0)  # unused
 
 
 # Normalization after processors
-register('store.normalize-after-processing', default=0.0)  # unused
-register('store.disable-trim-in-renormalization', default=0.0)  # unused
+register("store.normalize-after-processing", default=0.0)  # unused
+register("store.disable-trim-in-renormalization", default=0.0)  # unused
 
 # Post Process Error Hook Sampling
-register('post-process.use-error-hook-sampling', default=False)  # unused
+register("post-process.use-error-hook-sampling", default=False)  # unused
 # From 0.0 to 1.0: Randomly enqueue process_resource_change task
-register('post-process.error-hook-sample-rate', default=0.0)  # unused
+register("post-process.error-hook-sample-rate", default=0.0)  # unused
 
 # Transaction events
 # True => kill switch to disable ingestion of transaction events for internal project.
-register('transaction-events.force-disable-internal-project', default=False)
+register("transaction-events.force-disable-internal-project", default=False)

+register("mail.reply-hostname", default="", flags=FLAG_ALLOW_EMPTY | FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK)
+register("mail.mailgun-api-key", default="", flags=FLAG_ALLOW_EMPTY | FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK)
+register("mail.timeout", default=10, type=Int, flags=FLAG_ALLOW_EMPTY | FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK)
+
+# SMS
+register("sms.twilio-account", default="", flags=FLAG_ALLOW_EMPTY | FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK)
+register("sms.twilio-token", default="", flags=FLAG_ALLOW_EMPTY | FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK)

+
+# U2F

+
+register("auth.ip-rate-limit", default=0, flags=FLAG_ALLOW_EMPTY | FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK)
+register("auth.user-rate-limit", default=0, flags=FLAG_ALLOW_EMPTY | FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK)
+register(
+    "auth.allow-registration",
+    default=False,
+    flags=FLAG_ALLOW_EMPTY | FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK | FLAG_REQUIRED,
+)
+
+register("api.rate-limit.org-create", default=5, flags=FLAG_ALLOW_EMPTY | FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK)

+register("store.projects-normalize-in-rust-percent-opt-in", default=0.0)  # unused

 # Symbolicator refactors

 # Transaction events

introduced 4d5f54fe8Filippo Pacifici2020-01-02feat(eventstream): Register option to allow eventstream to send event to different topics (#16072)
commit 4d5f54fe853b99a692f9026d4f704ab561f353cb
Author: Filippo Pacifici <fpacifici@sentry.io>
Date:   Thu Jan 2 15:12:13 2020 -0800
    feat(eventstream): Register option to allow eventstream to send event to different topics (#16072)
    We are going to split transactions from other types of events in eventstore. They will go into two separate topics. This is to reduce the pressure on the event topic itself that kept backlogging on some partitions lately.

@@ -162,8 +162,12 @@ register("store.projects-normalize-in-rust-percent-opt-in", default=0.0)  # unus
 
 # From 0.0 to 1.0: Randomly disable normalization code in interfaces when loading from db
 register("store.empty-interface-sample-rate", default=0.0)
 
+# Enable multiple topics for eventstream. It allows specific event types to be sent
+# to specific topic.
+register("store.eventstream-per-type-topic", default=False, flags=FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK)
+
 # if this is turned to `True` sentry will behave like relay would do with
 # regards to filter responses.
 register("store.lie-about-filter-status", default=False)
SAFE_CANDIDATE store.experimental-dsn-double-write.sample-rate none
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:3180

register(
    "store.experimental-dsn-double-write.sample-rate",

no usages found
introduced c85504f38Shruthi2024-02-23chore: Add option for double write sample rate (#65718)
commit c85504f38d034e3d878a2c92ad53986853454a89
Author: Shruthi <shruthilaya.jaganathan@sentry.io>
Date:   Fri Feb 23 15:35:47 2024 -0500
    chore: Add option for double write sample rate (#65718)
    As we wait on SDKs to send us standalone spans, we can test the
    spans-only pipeline
    by just not producing to the transactions kafka topic in relay for a
    project.
    This will allow us to test and see what breaks when we only send spans.
    I'd like to double write the `sentry` backend project to an experimental
    dsn and switch
    that experimental project to exclusively only produce to the spans topic
    in relay
    (ie no transactions). Double writing so that sentry prod data doesn't
    break, and we
    can still test on production data and compare `sentry` and
    `sentry_experimental`
    projects product experiences.
    Adding a flag that will allow us to control the rollout of the double
    write.
    used in
    https://github.com/getsentry/sentry/pull/65719/files?diff=split&w=1

 register("store.use-relay-dsn-sample-rate", default=1)
 
 # Mock out integrations and services for tests

+
+# Record statistics about event payloads and their compressability
+register("store.nodestore-stats-sample-rate", default=0)

# The ratio of symbolication requests for which metrics will be submitted to redis.
#
# This is to allow gradual rollout of metrics collection for symbolication requests and can be
# removed once it is fully rolled out.
register(
    "symbolicate-event.low-priority.metrics.submission-rate",
    default=0.0,
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)

Author: Sebastian Zivota <loewenheim@users.noreply.github.com>

+

introduced 6f9d3126cArpad Borsos2023-07-26Hook up Bundle Index to JS Symbolication (#53058)
commit 6f9d3126c380a4b1c8c1a425b84a6604dc4088d5
Author: Arpad Borsos <arpad.borsos@sentry.io>
Date:   Wed Jul 26 10:53:44 2023 +0200
    Hook up Bundle Index to JS Symbolication (#53058)
    This sends new `debug_id_index` and `url_index` download links to
    Symbolicator which will use those to fetch artifact bundles based on the
    index.
    Also adds support to download the bundle index via the
    `artifact-lookup?download` API.

+register("snuba.search.max-total-chunk-time-seconds", default=30.0)
+register("snuba.search.hits-sample-size", default=100)
+register("snuba.track-outcomes-sample-rate", default=0.0)
 
 # Kafka Publisher
-register('kafka-publisher.raw-event-sample-rate', default=0.0)
-register('kafka-publisher.max-event-size', default=100000)
+register("kafka-publisher.raw-event-sample-rate", default=0.0)
+register("kafka-publisher.max-event-size", default=100000)
 
 # Ingest refactor
-register('store.projects-normalize-in-rust-opt-in', type=Sequence, default=[])  # unused
-register('store.projects-normalize-in-rust-opt-out', type=Sequence, default=[])  # unused
+register("store.projects-normalize-in-rust-opt-in", type=Sequence, default=[])  # unused
+register("store.projects-normalize-in-rust-opt-out", type=Sequence, default=[])  # unused
 # positive value means stable opt-in in the range 0.0 to 1.0, negative value
 # means random opt-in with the same range.
-register('store.projects-normalize-in-rust-percent-opt-in', default=0.0)  # unused
+register("store.projects-normalize-in-rust-percent-opt-in", default=0.0)  # unused
 
 # From 0.0 to 1.0: Randomly disable normalization code in interfaces when loading from db
-register('store.empty-interface-sample-rate', default=0.0)
+register("store.empty-interface-sample-rate", default=0.0)
 
 # Symbolicator refactors
 # - Disabling minidump stackwalking in endpoints
-register('symbolicator.minidump-refactor-projects-opt-in', type=Sequence, default=[])  # unused
-register('symbolicator.minidump-refactor-projects-opt-out', type=Sequence, default=[])  # unused
-register('symbolicator.minidump-refactor-random-sampling', default=0.0)  # unused
+register("symbolicator.minidump-refactor-projects-opt-in", type=Sequence, default=[])  # unused
+register("symbolicator.minidump-refactor-projects-opt-out", type=Sequence, default=[])  # unused
+register("symbolicator.minidump-refactor-random-sampling", default=0.0)  # unused
 
 
 # Normalization after processors
-register('store.normalize-after-processing', default=0.0)  # unused
-register('store.disable-trim-in-renormalization', default=0.0)  # unused
+register("store.normalize-after-processing", default=0.0)  # unused
+register("store.disable-trim-in-renormalization", default=0.0)  # unused
 
 # Post Process Error Hook Sampling
-register('post-process.use-error-hook-sampling', default=False)  # unused
+register("post-process.use-error-hook-sampling", default=False)  # unused
 # From 0.0 to 1.0: Randomly enqueue process_resource_change task
-register('post-process.error-hook-sample-rate', default=0.0)  # unused
+register("post-process.error-hook-sample-rate", default=0.0)  # unused
 
 # Transaction events
 # True => kill switch to disable ingestion of transaction events for internal project.
-register('transaction-events.force-disable-internal-project', default=False)
+register("transaction-events.force-disable-internal-project", default=False)

introduced 27cd775e5Tony Xiao2024-04-12feat(trace-explorer): New endpoint for search traces (#68733)
commit 27cd775e54335cedc21880b8c4d6a60662116a07
Author: Tony Xiao <txiao@sentry.io>
Date:   Fri Apr 12 10:41:05 2024 -0400
    feat(trace-explorer): New endpoint for search traces (#68733)
    There are some limitations around how the existing events endpoint works
    that makes loading traces for the trace explorer difficult. This
    encapsulates that logic.

src/sentry/options/defaults.py:3692
# Disables specific uptime checker regions. This is a list of region slugs
# which must match regions available in the settings.UPTIME_REGIONS list.
#
# Useful to remove a region from check rotation if there is some kind of
# problem with the region.
register(
    "uptime.disabled-checker-regions",
    type=Sequence,
    default=[],
    flags=FLAG_ALLOW_EMPTY | FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)

introduced 56b0ee5f2Kyle Consalus2025-09-26feat(aci): Precise project ID removal from Redis (#99852)
commit 56b0ee5f2bb57290aea7774f740ea32c9f71833c
Author: Kyle Consalus <kyle.consalus@sentry.io>
Date:   Fri Sep 26 13:05:49 2025 -0700
    feat(aci): Precise project ID removal from Redis (#99852)
    Rather than clearing out all project IDs in a range, this adds precise
    removal via Lua script.
    This isn't important currently, but is a prerequisite for partial
    project scheduling that is planned for load smoothing.

# List of categories to backfill new categories for.
# Each category should be a string representing the category name.
# The category name should be the same as the name in the DataCategory enum.
# Example: ["PROFILE_DURATION", "PROFILE_DURATION_UI"]
options.register(
    "backfill_new_categories.categories",
    default=[],
    type=Sequence,
    flags=options.FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)

# Control whether the backfill_new_categories task should run.

# If set to False, the task will not run.

    type=Bool,
    flags=options.FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)

# Control the rollout of the unified pipeline optimization for batch_incr().
# When enabled, all HINCRBY commands across all projects are sent in a single
# ClusterPipeline.execute() call instead of one pipeline per project.
options.register(
    "billing.usagebuffer.unified_pipeline.rollout",
    default=0.0,
    flags=options.FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)

src/sentry/options/defaults.py:3996
# Lists all the consumers we want to dump the stacktrace upon shutdown.
# We have seen some consumers hanging after restart. This should help
# us to identify where they get stuck.
# The consumer name is the ones defined in sentry.consumers.KAFKA_CONSUMERS.
register(
    "consumer.dump_stacktrace_on_shutdown",
    type=Sequence,
    default=[],
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (1)
src/sentry/runner/commands/run.py

)

NEVER_DELETE consumer.shared_memory_spawn_process both
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:4019
# Forces ArroyoRunTaskWithMultiprocessing steps to instruct the SharedMemoryManager to
# spawn processes rather than forking.
# As this impacts the shared memory manager initialization, which happens during
# the creation of the strategy, a rebalance or a restart is needed for this
# option change to take effect.
register(
    "consumer.shared_memory_spawn_process",
    type=Bool,
    default=False,
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (1)
src/sentry/utils/arroyo.py
NEVER_DELETE consumer.verbose_multiprocessing_logs string
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:4007
# Lists all the consumers we need verbose multiprocess logs for.
# We observed some consumers hanging after restarts. We narrowed down the
# issue to the shared memory manager initialization. Specifically,
# the consumer hangs when the shared memory manager initializes a subprocess.
register(
    "consumer.verbose_multiprocessing_logs",
    type=Sequence,
    default=[],
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)

NEVER_DELETE crons.per_monitor_rate_limit both
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:2630
# Determines how many check-ins per-minute will be allowed per monitor. This is
# used when computing the QuotaConfig for the DataCategory.MONITOR (check-ins)
#
# See the sentry.monitors.rate_limt module for more details.
#
# XXX(epurkhiser): Remember a single check-in may often consist of two check-in
# messages, one for IN_PROGRESS and another for OK.
register(
    "crons.per_monitor_rate_limit",
    type=Int,
    default=6,
    flags=FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK | FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (4)
src/sentry/monitors/consumers/monitor_consumer.pysrc/sentry/monitors/rate_limit.pytests/sentry/monitors/consumers/test_monitor_consumer.pytests/sentry/monitors/test_rate_limit.py

# Enables recording tick volume metrics and tick decisions based on those
# metrics. Decisions are used to delay notifications in a system incident.
register(
    "crons.system_incidents.collect_metrics",
    default=False,
    flags=FLAG_BOOL | FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)

src/sentry/monitors/system_incidents.pytests/sentry/monitors/test_clock_dispatch.pytests/sentry/monitors/test_system_incidents.py
NEVER_DELETE crons.system_incidents.pct_deviation_anomaly_threshold both
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:2601
# The threshold that the tick metric must surpass for a tick to be determined
# as anomalous. This value should be negative, since we will only determine an
# incident based on a decrease in volume.
#
# See the `monitors.system_incidents` module for more details
register(
    "crons.system_incidents.pct_deviation_anomaly_threshold",
    default=-10,
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (2)
src/sentry/monitors/system_incidents.pytests/sentry/monitors/test_system_incidents.py
NEVER_DELETE crons.system_incidents.pct_deviation_incident_threshold both
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:2609
# The threshold that the tick metric must surpass to transition to an incident
# state. This should be a fairly high value to avoid false positive incidents.
register(
    "crons.system_incidents.pct_deviation_incident_threshold",
    default=-30,
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (2)
src/sentry/monitors/system_incidents.pytests/sentry/monitors/test_system_incidents.py
NEVER_DELETE crons.system_incidents.tick_decision_window both

# tick decisions for to determine a decision about the tick being evaluated.
register(
    "crons.system_incidents.tick_decision_window",
    default=5,
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)

src/sentry/monitors/system_incidents.pytests/sentry/monitors/test_system_incidents.py

# Enables the the crons incident occurrence consumer to consider the clock-tick

# should be processed, delayed, or dropped entirely.

)

# Database field encryption method
# Supported values:
# - 'plaintext': No encryption (default)
# - 'fernet': Fernet symmetric encryption
# - 'keysets': (Future) Google Tink keysets for key rotation

    "database.encryption.method",
    type=String,
    default="plaintext",

)

src/sentry/options/defaults.py:2416
# Enables a feature flag check in dynamic sampling tasks that switches
# organizations between transactions and spans for rebalancing. This check is
# expensive, so it can be disabled using this option.
register(
    "dynamic-sampling.check_span_feature_flag",
    default=False,
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE | FLAG_MODIFIABLE_RATE,
)
usages (6)
src/sentry/core/endpoints/organization_details.pysrc/sentry/dynamic_sampling/per_org/tasks/configuration.pysrc/sentry/dynamic_sampling/tasks/boost_low_volume_projects.pytests/sentry/dynamic_sampling/per_org/tasks/test_configuration.pytests/sentry/dynamic_sampling/tasks/test_boost_low_volume_projects.pytests/sentry/dynamic_sampling/tasks/test_tasks.py
NEVER_DELETE dynamic-sampling.config.killswitch both
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:2370
# Stops dynamic sampling rules from being emitted in relay config.
# This is required for ST instances that have flakey flags as we want to be able kill DS ruining customer data if necessary.
# It is only a killswitch for behaviour, it may actually increase infra load if flipped for a user currently being sampled.
register("dynamic-sampling.config.killswitch", default=False, flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE)
usages (1)
src/sentry/relay/config/__init__.py
NEVER_DELETE dynamic-sampling.measure.spans both

# List of organization IDs that should be using spans for rebalancing in dynamic sampling.
register(
    "dynamic-sampling.measure.spans",
    default=[],
    type=Sequence,
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (6)
src/sentry/core/endpoints/organization_details.pysrc/sentry/dynamic_sampling/per_org/tasks/configuration.pysrc/sentry/dynamic_sampling/tasks/boost_low_volume_projects.pytests/sentry/dynamic_sampling/per_org/tasks/test_configuration.pytests/sentry/dynamic_sampling/tasks/test_boost_low_volume_projects.pytests/sentry/dynamic_sampling/tasks/test_tasks.py
NEVER_DELETE dynamic-sampling.per_org.killswitch string
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:2374
# Killswitch for the per-org dynamic sampling pipeline. When set to True, the
# scheduled entry point exits before it can enqueue work.
register(
    "dynamic-sampling.per_org.killswitch",
    default=False,
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (3)
src/sentry/dynamic_sampling/per_org/tasks/gate.pytests/sentry/dynamic_sampling/per_org/tasks/test_scheduler.pytests/sentry/dynamic_sampling/per_org/tasks/test_telemetry.py
NEVER_DELETE dynamic-sampling.per_org.metrics-sample-rate string
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:2395
# Sample rate for metrics emitted by the per-org dynamic sampling pipeline
# (status counters, org_status counters, duration timer). 1.0 emits every
# event; lower values drop events proportionally. Use this to reduce metric
# volume/cost when the pipeline is rolled out to many organizations.
register(
    "dynamic-sampling.per_org.metrics-sample-rate",
    type=Float,
    default=1.0,
    flags=FLAG_MODIFIABLE_RATE | FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (2)
src/sentry/dynamic_sampling/per_org/tasks/gate.pytests/sentry/dynamic_sampling/per_org/tasks/test_telemetry.py
NEVER_DELETE dynamic-sampling.per_org.rollout-rate string
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:2384
# Deterministic % rollout of the per-org dynamic sampling pipeline, keyed on
# organization id. A value of 0.0 disables the pipeline for every org; 1.0
# enables it for every org. Intermediate values select a stable hash-based
# subset so toggling the rate up and down does not reshuffle which orgs run.
register(
    "dynamic-sampling.per_org.rollout-rate",
    type=Float,
    default=0.0,
    flags=FLAG_MODIFIABLE_RATE | FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (3)
src/sentry/dynamic_sampling/per_org/tasks/gate.pytests/sentry/dynamic_sampling/per_org/tasks/test_scheduler.pytests/sentry/dynamic_sampling/per_org/tasks/test_telemetry.py

# Number of large transactions to retrieve from Snuba for transaction re-balancing.
register(
    "dynamic-sampling.prioritise_transactions.num_explicit_large_transactions",
    30,
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (2)
src/sentry/dynamic_sampling/tasks/boost_low_volume_transactions.pytests/sentry/dynamic_sampling/tasks/test_tasks.py
NEVER_DELETE dynamic-sampling.prioritise_transactions.num_explicit_small_transactions both
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:2361
# Number of large transactions to retrieve from Snuba for transaction re-balancing.
register(
    "dynamic-sampling.prioritise_transactions.num_explicit_small_transactions",
    0,
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (2)
src/sentry/dynamic_sampling/tasks/boost_low_volume_transactions.pytests/sentry/dynamic_sampling/tasks/test_tasks.py
NEVER_DELETE dynamic-sampling.prioritise_transactions.rebalance_intensity both
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:2407
# Controls the intensity of dynamic sampling transaction rebalancing. 0.0 = explict rebalancing
# not performed, 1.0= full rebalancing (tries to bring everything to mean). Note that even at 0.0
# there will still be some rebalancing between the explicit and implicit transactions ( so setting rebalancing
# to 0.0 is not the same as no rebalancing. To effectively disable rebalancing set the number of explicit
# transactions to be rebalance (both small and large) to 0.
register(
    "dynamic-sampling.prioritise_transactions.rebalance_intensity",
    default=0.8,
    flags=FLAG_MODIFIABLE_RATE | FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (2)
src/sentry/dynamic_sampling/tasks/boost_low_volume_transactions.pytests/sentry/dynamic_sampling/tasks/test_tasks.py
NEVER_DELETE dynamic-sampling:sliding_window.size both
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:2353
# Dynamic Sampling system-wide options
# Size of the sliding window used for dynamic sampling. It is defaulted to 24 hours.
register("dynamic-sampling:sliding_window.size", default=24, flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE)

# Used in single-tenants to allowlist flagpole features in tenants.
# This allows incremental adoption of flagpole in single tenant
# as we validate feature flag compatibility in those environments.
options.register(
    "flagpole.allowed_features",
    default=["*"],
    type=Sequence,
    flags=options.FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)

src/sentry/options/defaults.py:3159
# This is here in case we want to roll out a new config gradually. Can also be used as a killswitch
# for config upgrades if one is ever needed.
register(
    "grouping.config_transition.config_upgrade_sample_rate",
    type=Float,
    default=1.0,
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (2)
src/sentry/grouping/ingest/config.pytests/sentry/grouping/test_config.py
NEVER_DELETE grouping.config_transition.metrics_sample_rate both
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:3150
# TODO: For now, only a small number of projects are going through a grouping config transition at
# any given time, so we're sampling at 100% in order to be able to get good signal. Once we've fully
# transitioned to the optimized logic, and before the next config change, we probably either want to
# turn this down or get rid of it in favor of the default 10% sample rate
register(
    "grouping.config_transition.metrics_sample_rate",
    type=Float,
    default=1.0,
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (2)
src/sentry/grouping/ingest/config.pysrc/sentry/grouping/ingest/metrics.py

NEVER_DELETE grouping.use_ingest_grouphash_caching both
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:3171
# When handling grouphashes during ingest, use the cache to reduce postgres load. With secondary
# grouphashes we want to use ones which are already there but not create new ones, so we track the
# boolean result of their `.exists()` check. For all existing grouphashes, secondary or not, we know
# that if they already have a group assigned they won't be modified, so in that case we also cache
# the full `GroupHash` object.
register(
    "grouping.use_ingest_grouphash_caching",
    type=Bool,
    default=True,
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (5)
src/sentry/grouping/ingest/caching.pysrc/sentry/grouping/ingest/hashing.pysrc/sentry/models/grouphash.pytests/sentry/event_manager/test_event_manager_grouping.pytests/sentry/models/test_grouphash.py

NEVER_DELETE issues.search.use-tag-aware-condition-resolver both
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:3777
# Routes the seen-stats / TSDB conditions through `get_snuba_column_name`
# so a column-name-vs-tag-name collision (e.g. user tag named `platform`)
# resolves to the tag, matching the issue surfacing query. Without this,
# `resolve_column`'s DATASET_FIELDS shortcut treats user-typed bare column
# names as column references and the badge disagrees with surfacing.
register(
    "issues.search.use-tag-aware-condition-resolver",
    type=Bool,
    default=False,
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (10)

# Send event messages for specific project IDs to random partitions in Kafka
# contents are a list of project IDs to message types to be randomly assigned
# e.g. [{"project_id": 2, "message_type": "error"}, {"project_id": 3, "message_type": "transaction"}]
register(
    "kafka.send-project-events-to-random-partitions",
    default=[],
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)

NEVER_DELETE nodestore.cache-ttl both
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:2512
# TTL in seconds for nodestore cache entries. Event bodies are immutable
# so longer TTLs are safe and improve cache hit rates for batch reads
# (e.g. group events list endpoint).
register(
    "nodestore.cache-ttl",
    default=300,
    type=Int,
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (2)
src/sentry/services/nodestore/base.pytests/sentry/services/nodestore/test_common.py

src/sentry/options/defaults.py:3468
# Killswitch list of NotificationSource values that should be blocked from being
# dispatched by the notification platform's NotificationService. Values must match
# the string values of `sentry.notifications.platform.types.NotificationSource`.
register(
    "notifications.platform.killswitch.sources",
    type=Sequence,
    default=[],
    flags=FLAG_ALLOW_EMPTY | FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (1)

NEVER_DELETE objectstore.config both
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:420
# New `objectstore` service configuration. Additional supported options and
# their defaults:
#  - propagate_traces: bool = False,
#  - retries: int | None = None,
#  - timeout_ms: float | None = None,
#  - connection_kwargs: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
#  - token_generator: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
#
# For an always up-to-date list, see:
# https://getsentry.github.io/objectstore/python/objectstore_client.html#objectstore_client.Client
register(
    "objectstore.config",
    default={
        "base_url": "http://127.0.0.1:8888",
        # Test-only token generator with no permissions. Only active when no real
        # objectstore config is deployed. Exists so mint_token() does not raise in
        # test/dev environments that lack signing keys.
        "token_generator": {
            "kid": "test",
            "secret_key": "-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----\nMC4CAQAwBQYDK2VwBCIEIOrZqzixETRBXsZl85d83N5nwb71ctTZ3/mwu1TX90vG\n-----END PRIVATE KEY-----\n",
            "permissions": [],
        },
    },
    flags=FLAG_NOSTORE,
)
usages (5)

NEVER_DELETE project-abuse-quota.error-limit string
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:1674
# These are set to 0: don't limit by default.
# These have yet to be configured in production.
# For errors and transactions, the above DEPRECATED options take
# precedence for now, until we decide on values to set for all these.
# Set the same key as an org option which will override these values for the org.
# Similarly, for now, the DEPRECATED org options "sentry:project-error-limit"
# and "sentry:project-transaction-limit" take precedence.
register(
    "project-abuse-quota.error-limit",
    type=Int,
    default=0,
    flags=FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK | FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)

NEVER_DELETE project-abuse-quota.window both
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:1641
# Relay isn't effective at enforcing 1s windows - 10 seconds has worked well.
# If the limit is negative, then it means completely blocked.
# I don't see this value needing to be tweaked on a per-org basis,
# so for now the org option "project-abuse-quota.window" doesn't do anything.
register(
    "project-abuse-quota.window",
    type=Int,
    default=10,
    flags=FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK | FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (2)

# When True, schedule_invalidate_project_config calls the invalidation callback
# directly when outside an atomic block, instead of going through
# transaction.on_commit(). This fixes TransactionManagementError in the
# taskworker where autocommit is off.
register(
    "relay.invalidation-direct-outside-atomic",
    default=False,
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)

NEVER_DELETE relocation.enabled both
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:2975
# Relocation: whether or not the self-serve API for the feature is enabled. When set on a region
# silo, this flag controls whether or not that region's API will serve relocation requests to
# non-superuser clients. When set on the control silo, it can be used to regulate whether or not
# certain global UI (ex: the relocation creation form at `/relocation/`) is visible to users.
register(
    "relocation.enabled",
    default=False,
    flags=FLAG_BOOL | FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)

# Relocation: populates the target region drop down in the control silo. Note: this option has NO
# EFFECT in region silos. However, the control silos `relocation.selectable-regions` array should be
# a complete list of all regions where `relocation.enabled`. If a region is enabled/disabled, it
# should also be added to/removed from this array in the control silo at the same time.
register(
    "relocation.selectable-regions",
    default=[],
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)

NEVER_DELETE seer.similarity-killswitch.enabled both
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:1169
# Killswitches for individual Seer services
#
# TODO: Most of these are not yet being used. The one current exception is the similarity service
# killswitch, which is checked before calling Seer when potentially creating a  new group as part of
# ingestion.
register(
    "seer.similarity-killswitch.enabled",
    default=False,
    type=Bool,
    flags=FLAG_MODIFIABLE_BOOL | FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (2)
src/sentry/seer/similarity/utils.pytests/sentry/grouping/seer_similarity/test_seer_eligibility.py
NEVER_DELETE seer.similarity.circuit-breaker-config both
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:1278
# Note: This is based on US volume. Since other regions are lower-traffic, this effectively means
# the circuit breaker is disabled for any region without its own values configured (you can hardly
# have 33K Seer errors if you don't even have 33K events, so the breaker will never be tripped in
# smaller regions relying on the default).
register(
    "seer.similarity.circuit-breaker-config",
    type=Dict,
    default={
        "error_limit": 33250,  # 95% error rate * avg volume of ~35K events with new hashes/10 min
        "error_limit_window": 600,  # 10 min
        "broken_state_duration": 300,  # 5 min
    },
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (2)
src/sentry/grouping/ingest/seer.pysrc/sentry/seer/similarity/similar_issues.py

    flags=FLAG_ALLOW_EMPTY | FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)

)

src/sentry/utils/sentry_apps/webhooks.pytests/sentry/utils/sentry_apps/test_webhook_timeout.py
NEVER_DELETE sentry-apps.webhook.circuit-breaker.config both
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:2657
# Circuit breaker configuration for webhook endpoint failure detection.
# Keys match RateBasedTripStrategyConfig + CircuitBreakerConfig
register(
    "sentry-apps.webhook.circuit-breaker.config",
    type=Dict,
    default={
        "error_limit_window": 600,  # 10 minutes
        "broken_state_duration": 300,  # 5 minutes
        "threshold": 0.5,  # 50% error rate
        "floor": 500,  # 500 errors before error rate check applies
        "metrics_key": "sentry-app.webhook",  # to avoid high cardinality slug tag
    },
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (2)
src/sentry/utils/sentry_apps/webhooks.pytests/sentry/utils/sentry_apps/test_webhooks.py
NEVER_DELETE sentry-apps.webhook.hard-timeout.sec both
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:2649
# Hard timeout for webhook requests to prevent indefinite hangs.
# Must be strictly less than the shortest task processing_deadline_duration that
# calls send_and_save_webhook_request (currently 8s for send_alert_webhook_v2 and
# send_resource_change_webhook), otherwise timeout_alarm raises ValueError.
register(
    "sentry-apps.webhook.hard-timeout.sec",
    default=5.0,
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (2)
src/sentry/utils/sentry_apps/webhooks.pytests/sentry/utils/sentry_apps/test_webhook_timeout.py

register(

    type=Sequence,
    default=[],
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)

NEVER_DELETE sentry-metrics.writes-limiter.limits.performance.per-org both
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:1836
# Global and per-organization limits on the writes to the string indexer's DB.
#
# Format is a list of dictionaries of format {
#   "window_seconds": ...,
#   "granularity_seconds": ...,
#   "limit": ...
# }
#
# See sentry.ratelimiters.sliding_windows for an explanation of what each of
# those terms mean.
#
# Note that changing either window or granularity_seconds of a limit will
# effectively reset it, as the previous data can't/won't be converted.
register(
    "sentry-metrics.writes-limiter.limits.performance.per-org",
    default=[],
    flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (1)
src/sentry/sentry_metrics/indexer/limiters/writes.py

# Compression level for spans buffer segments. Default -1 disables compression, 0-22 for zstd levels
register(
    "spans.buffer.compression.level",
    type=Int,
    default=0,
    flags=FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK | FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)

src/sentry/spans/buffer.pytests/sentry/spans/test_buffer.py
NEVER_DELETE spans.buffer.debug-traces both
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:3348
# List of trace_ids to enable debug logging for. Empty = debug off.
# When set, logs detailed metrics about zunionstore set sizes, key existence, and trace structure.
register(
    "spans.buffer.debug-traces",
    type=Sequence,
    default=[],
    flags=FLAG_ALLOW_EMPTY | FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (3)
src/sentry/spans/debug_trace_logger.pytests/sentry/spans/test_buffer.pytests/sentry/spans/test_debug_trace_logger.py
NEVER_DELETE spans.buffer.done-flush-conditional-zrem string
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:3294
# When True, done_flush_segments uses Lua scripts to conditionally clean up
# segments only if their state hasn't changed since the flush started: Phase 1
# ZREMs the queue entry only if the score is unchanged, and Phase 2 deletes the
# per-segment data keys (hrs, ic, ibc) only if the ingested count is unchanged.
# When False, both phases unconditionally remove queue entries and delete data
# keys for every flushed segment.
register(
    "spans.buffer.done-flush-conditional-zrem",
    default=True,
    flags=FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK | FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)

# Number of seconds the flusher needs to be saturated before we issue backpressure
register(
    "spans.buffer.flusher.backpressure-seconds",
    default=10,
    flags=FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK | FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (2)
src/sentry/spans/consumers/process/flusher.pytests/sentry/spans/test_buffer.py
NEVER_DELETE spans.buffer.flusher.flush-lock-ttl both
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:3281
# TTL (in seconds) for the per-segment lock acquired at flush time to
# prevent two flushers from producing the same segment concurrently.
# The lock is never explicitly released and only expires via this TTL.
# Pick a value larger than the expected flush+produce latency but smaller than
# `spans.buffer.root-timeout` so a re-entered segment isn't blocked from its
# next flush cycle. If set to 0, no locks will be acquired.
register(
    "spans.buffer.flusher.flush-lock-ttl",
    type=Int,
    default=0,
    flags=FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK | FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (2)
src/sentry/spans/buffer.pytests/sentry/spans/test_buffer.py
NEVER_DELETE spans.buffer.flusher.log-flushed-segments both
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:3340

    default=False,
    flags=FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK | FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (2)

    default=60,

)

# Maximum number of spans per EVALSHA call. Large subsegments are split into
# chunks to avoid Lua unpack() limits. Set to 0 for unlimited.

    "spans.buffer.max-spans-per-evalsha",
    type=Int,
    default=0,
    flags=FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK | FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)

src/sentry/options/defaults.py:3309
# Maximum number of subsegments to process in each Redis pipeline. Each
# subsegment triggers an EVALSHA call which can be slow. Set to 0 for unlimited.
register(
    "spans.buffer.pipeline-batch-size",
    type=Int,
    default=0,
    flags=FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK | FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)

NEVER_DELETE spans.process-segments.dedupe-ttl both
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:3385
# TTL in seconds for span deduplication tracking. When > 0, the consumer
# will use Redis SETNX to detect duplicate spans and emit metrics.
# Set to 0 to disable.
register(
    "spans.process-segments.dedupe-ttl",
    type=Int,
    default=0,
    flags=FLAG_PRIORITIZE_DISK | FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)

NEVER_DELETE store.use-relay-dsn-sample-rate both
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:1051
# Killswitch for sending internal errors to the internal project or
# `SENTRY_SDK_CONFIG.relay_dsn`. Set to `0` to only send to
# `SENTRY_SDK_CONFIG.dsn` (the "upstream transport") and nothing else.
#
# Note: A value that is neither 0 nor 1 is regarded as 0
register("store.use-relay-dsn-sample-rate", default=1, flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE)

# The ratio of events for which we emit verbose apple symbol stats.

# This is to allow collecting more information on why symx is not performing as it should.

# The list of specific os_name+os_version for which we log extra infromation.
#
# This is done since SYMX is not performing bad across the board but rather only in specific case (what we are interested in).

# Killswitch for symbolication sources, based on a list of source IDs. Meant to be used in extreme
# situations where it is preferable to break symbolication in a few places as opposed to letting
# it break everywhere.
register(
    "symbolicator.ignored_sources",
    type=Sequence,
    default=[],
    flags=FLAG_ALLOW_EMPTY | FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)

NEVER_DELETE tempest.lock-buffer-seconds both
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:1616
# Extra buffer time (in seconds) added to task deadline for lock duration.
# Lock duration = task-deadline-seconds + lock-buffer-seconds
# This ensures the lock outlives the task to prevent race conditions.
register("tempest.lock-buffer-seconds", default=30, flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE)
usages (2)
src/sentry/tempest/tasks.pytests/sentry/tempest/test_tempest.py

# The poll limit for the tempest service.
#
# 25 every 1 min ~ 36k per day
register("tempest.poll-limit", default=25, flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE)

NEVER_DELETE tempest.task-deadline-seconds both
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:1611
# Task processing deadline in seconds (for documentation/monitoring reference).
# Note: The actual @instrumented_task decorator requires compile-time constants,
# so changing this option won't affect running tasks. This documents the expected
# value and can be used for configuring monitoring alerts.
register("tempest.task-deadline-seconds", default=60, flags=FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE)

NEVER_DELETE uptime.restrict-issue-creation-by-hosting-provider-id both
src/sentry/options/defaults.py:3680
# Restrict uptime issue creation for specific host provider identifiers. Items
# in this list map to the `host_provider_id` column in the UptimeSubscription
# table.
#
# This may be used to stop issue creation in the event that a network / hosting
# provider blocks the uptime checker causing false positives.
register(
    "uptime.restrict-issue-creation-by-hosting-provider-id",
    type=Sequence,
    default=[],
    flags=FLAG_ALLOW_EMPTY | FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (2)

# Configures the list of public IP addresses that are returned from the

# list without the need for a code-change.

NEVER_DELETE vercel.invoice-notpaid.disable-downgrade both
getsentry/models/__init__.py:81
# When True, the invoice.notpaid webhook will skip the downgrade logic (close invoice
# + cancel plan). Use this once the invoice.overdue webhook is live, since invoice.overdue
# fires after payment retries are exhausted, unlike invoice.notpaid which fires immediately.
options.register(
    "vercel.invoice-notpaid.disable-downgrade",
    default=False,
    flags=FLAG_MODIFIABLE_BOOL | options.FLAG_AUTOMATOR_MODIFIABLE,
)
usages (2)
getsentry/web/vercel/webhooks/invoice_notpaid.pytests/getsentry/web/vercel/webhooks/test_invoice_notpaid.py

register(
    "viewer-context.enabled",
    default=True,
    type=Bool,
    flags=FLAG_NOSTORE,
)
